requirements.notes: Daily OHLCV bars available from 2016-present via Alpaca. Covers entry/exit pricing, volume filters, return calculation, and all derived OHLCV metrics needed for the strategy.

IPO date is the core signal input. fundamentals_basic provides current-snapshot metadata via Alpaca asset metadata (market cap, ADV, listing info). It is NOT point-in-time. Survivorship bias applies: delisted, acquired, or SPAC-converted tickers will be missing. Historical IPO date accuracy for spin-offs and direct listings is uncertain. This is the primary data quality risk for the strategy.

fields.title: Anniversary Idea
fields.thesis: We buy stocks that are currently having their birthday. If the company IPO'd on 05/03/1993 we will buy it on 05/03 of the present year.
fields.signal_logic: IPO Anniversary Signal: On each trading day, identify all stocks in the universe whose IPO date shares the same month and day as the current calendar date (MM-DD match). These stocks are flagged as 'anniversary candidates' for that session.
fields.entry_rules: {"trigger":"Signal fires at market open on the anniversary trading day. If the anniversary date falls on a weekend or holiday, the signal fires on the next valid trading day.","order_type":"market_on_open","position_sizing":"Equal-weight across all anniversary candidates on a given day. Maximum 5% of portfolio per position to prevent concentration on days with few candidates.","confirmation_filter":"None required for baseline test. Optional v2 filter: require 20-day average daily volume > 500,000 shares to exclude illiquid names.","max_concurrent_positions":20,"notes":"On days with zero anniversary candidates, no trades are placed. On days with many candidates, the equal-weight cap applies."}
fields.exit_rules: {"primary_exit":"Close position at end of the same trading day (intraday hold). The anniversary effect, if real, is expected to be a single-session phenomenon.","stop_loss_pct":5,"target_pct":null,"hold_period":"1 trading day (same-session close)","notes":"A 5% intraday stop is included as a risk guardrail. No profit target is set for the baseline — the position exits at close regardless. A v2 variant could test a 2-day or 5-day hold to check if any effect persists."}

risk_model.description: {"position_limit_pct":5,"max_portfolio_heat_pct":25,"stop_loss_pct":5,"target_pct":null,"max_drawdown_abort_pct":15,"sizing_method":"equal_weight","notes":"Because anniversary candidates are sparse and calendar-driven, portfolio heat will typically be low. The 25% max heat cap prevents runaway exposure on rare days with many simultaneous anniversaries. The 15% portfolio drawdown abort is a circuit breaker for the backtest engine."}

decisive_verdict_rules.pass: Passes only if it beats the benchmark after meeting the minimum trade-count and evaluated-days thresholds.
decisive_verdict_rules.inconclusive: Trade count < 50 after running the full primary window. Expand universe to all Alpaca-tradeable US equities and re-run before rendering a verdict.
decisive_verdict_rules.fail: Fails if it underperforms the benchmark or breaches risk limits after evidence thresholds are met.

acceptance_criteria.other: Minimum 50 completed round-trip trades required before any acceptance or rejection verdict is rendered. If trade count is below 50, the result is inconclusive and the universe must be expanded.

known_limitations: CRITICAL — IPO date metadata: fundamentals_basic provides only a current snapshot via Alpaca asset metadata. It is NOT point-in-time. Survivorship bias is present: tickers that were acquired, delisted, or went private will be missing from the universe. This is a structural limitation of the current data catalog.

IPO date accuracy: For many tickers, especially those that listed via SPAC, direct listing, or spin-off, the 'IPO date' in metadata may reflect the listing date rather than the original offering date. The anniversary signal may fire on the wrong date for these names.

Trade count uncertainty: The actual number of anniversary matches per year depends entirely on how many tickers in the chosen universe have IPO dates that fall on trading days and are accurately recorded. This cannot be confirmed without running the data pull.
Same-day liquidity: The strategy enters and exits on the same day. For smaller names in the universe, same-day fills at open and close may carry meaningful slippage not captured in OHLCV backtest.

Operator context: The operator has stated this is a functional/plumbing test, not a live trading candidate. The experiment plan is drafted in good faith to validate the mechanics, but the thesis has no established academic or practitioner precedent and should be treated as exploratory.

No historical fundamentals: fundamentals_basic is a current snapshot only. Historical point-in-time IPO dates are not available via a wired catalog connector. The backtest will use today's metadata applied backward, which is a form of look-ahead for any ticker whose listing status changed.
